stories.headline: World Cup Ticket Prices in Mexico City Too Expensive for Most Fans
stories.summary: The opening game in Mexico City took place amid weeks of protests from groups including teachers’ unions complaining about pay and pensions.

stories.headline: Gaza residents find brief escape in World Cup opener
stories.summary: Palestinian Territories residents gathered in tents, cafes and makeshift community spaces on 11 June to watch the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, seeking a brief escape from the hardships of war.

stories.headline: Ukraine's drone strikes are methodically cutting Crimea off from Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine has significantly intensified its strikes on military and energy sites in and around Russia-occupied Crimea in what is seen as Kyiv’s deliberate strategy of cutting the annexed Ukrainian peninsula off from Russia.

stories.headline: Russian drone barrage hits Kyiv and Mykolaiv as Ukraine reports major fires
stories.summary: Russian drone strikes hit multiple locations across Ukraine overnight into 12 June, damaging sites near Kyiv and in the Mykolaiv region, injuring one person and triggering a major fire, while Ukrainian air defences said they intercepted most of the 117 drones launched.

stories.summary: Vanguard Group has overtaken BlackRock Inc. as the largest exchange-traded fund issuer, a seismic shift at the top of the $15.2 trillion US industry.

stories.headline: China’s Credit Growth Dips as Weak Demand Frustrates Policy Push
stories.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed in May from a year earlier, as private demand remained subdued even though regulators pushed banks to lend more.

stories.headline: ‘Extreme’ Rotations Leave Stock Market Bulls Without a Playbook

stories.headline: SpaceX Prepares for Debut After $75 Billion IPO Breaks Record

stories.headline: Ukraine Steel Giant Tests Investor Demand After Bond Redemption

stories.headline: Jakarta Plans Indonesia’s First Municipal Bond Within a Year

stories.headline: Banks Court Wealthy Indian Diaspora in $50 Billion Deposit Drive
stories.summary: After more than a decade, India is once again turning to its 35 million-strong diaspora for support. This time, banks are mounting a global campaign to attract more than $50 billion in deposits from overseas nationals as policymakers seek to bolster foreign exchange reserves and support a battered rupee.

stories.headline: Jakarta Police Clash With Students Protesting Prabowo Plans
stories.summary: Indonesian police clashed with students in central Jakarta Friday, trying to contain a protest against the rising cost of living and President Prabowo Subianto’s spending plans.

stories.headline: South Africa Treasury Says Upgrades Show Path to Investment Grade

stories.headline: Swiss Firms Expect Population Cap to Be Defanged, Lobby Says
stories.summary: Swiss companies expect the government to water down the proposed population cap if voters back it, according to the country’s main business lobby.

stories.summary: Stocks extended their rally while oil hit the lowest level in months following fresh evidence that the US and Iran are nearing a provisional agreement to end their war.

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya

Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.
